example: The eldest was solemn and beautiful.
The second was lively, eager, and was quick to please.
He possessed a quiet grace that made people trust him.
Rumours suggested they might try to undermine him.
He chose to distract his sons with vague promises.
The eldest rode east with a retinue of guards.
The second rode west with a caravan of merchants.
The youngest prince rode alone, guided only by instinct.
Hills that rode endlessly toward the horizon.
Seeking shelter, the prince urged his horse into a shadowed path.
A castle luminous and shimmery as if polished with moonlight.
The prince approached the gate and gnarled.
ja: ねじれた、こぶだらけの

example: Cats bowed with graceful courtesy.
Wore small velvet collars adorned with tiny gems.
A dignified cat gestured as any butler for him to follow.
Decorated with rich tapestries and portraits.
ja: タペストリー、壁掛け織物

example: A cat radiating an almost human intelligence.
Cats carrying trays and arranging dishes with surprising dexterity.
The table was set with delicacies worthy of a royal feast.
Escorted to an exquisitely prepared bedroom.
The prince found himself enveloped in comforts.
Filled with conversation, companionship, and longing.
I shall give you a gift that will surpass anything your brothers bring.
The prince obeyed the white cat's instructions.
ja: 従順な、言うことを聞く

example: The king was still reluctant to relinquish his crown.
He was still reluctant to relinquish his crown.
The prince felt a strange mixture of dread and hope.
Who could be more beautiful, more captivating than the white cat?
Only then will the enchantment end.
The white cat serene and fearless bowed her head.
In a burst of golden light, the small feline form dissolved.
A young woman stood before him, radiant and graceful.
zh: 光芒四射的，容光煥發的

example: The entire castle trembled, then transformed.
A magnificent procession arrived at the palace.
She had undeniable royal bearing.
They could not deny the fairness of the judgment.
The prince and princess were married in a ceremony.
They lived for many long and prosperous years.
ja: 繁栄している、成功した

example: I was cursed as an infant by spiteful fairies.
Brave enough to break the spell despite personal anguish.
Love, loyalty and patience can pierce enchantments.
